user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Implement 3-phase PWM motor control on TC387 using six single-output GTM TOM PWM channels at 20 kHz, center-aligned, with HS on P02.0/P02.1/P02.2 and LS on P02.7/P02.4/P02.5, HS initialized to 25/50/75% and updated every 500 ms by +10% with wrap; LS default is complementary (inverted) of HS with no dead-time.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: All six TOM0 channels share one time base for center-aligned PWM at 20 kHz. High-side duties start at 25/50/75% for phases U/V/W and increase by +10% every 500 ms with wrap to 0% after exceeding 100%. Low-side outputs mirror complementary behavior (inverted duty) with zero dead-time. Outputs are routed to P02.x TOUT pins per verified pin macros.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use a shared TOM0 time base via IfxGtm_Tom_Timer and manage six synchronized PWM outputs using the high-level IfxGtm_Pwm multi-channel API. Configure TOM0 channels 0/1/2/4/5/7 routed to P02.0/1/2/4/5/7 using IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout from the TC38x LFBGA292 pin map. Initialize frequency (20 kHz center-aligned) with the TOM timer and initialize six PWM channels in one IfxGtm_Pwm instance. Set initial HS duties (25/50/75%) and program LS as complementary by polarity configuration. Update duties every 500 ms using IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate with wrap, then apply the timer update where required. Add a pre-implementation validation step to confirm TOM instance/channel-to-TOUT pin mapping against the actual board pinout and IfxGtm_PinMap; defer final channel selection until this check passes.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: IfxGtm_Pwm provides unified high-level multi-channel PWM with synchronized updates, center-aligned operation, and per-channel duty and polarity control, which directly supports six TOM outputs on a shared time base and periodic duty updates.

test_support.abstraction_layer: Introduce a thin hardware-abstraction interface (GtmPwmIf) that wraps IfxGtm_Tom_Timer and IfxGtm_Pwm calls and is injected into GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM.c via function pointers.
test_support.mocking_strategy: Provide a mock GtmPwmIf for unit tests to simulate timer ticks and capture duty writes without GTM hardware.
test_support.unit_test_strategy: Drive GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update() with a simulated 500 ms tick; assert computed duties, polarity, and that applyUpdate is called after duty writes.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, increase HS duty by +10% with wrap and apply complementary LS behavior via configured polarity; push updates atomically to hardware.

api_boundary_clarification.timer_owner: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_init encapsulates all IfxGtm_Tom_Timer configuration and lifetime (time base, center-aligned 20 kHz, channel mask). No other module configures the timer.
api_boundary_clarification.channel_owner: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update manages all per-channel duty updates via IfxGtm_Pwm APIs and does not modify timer configuration.
update_flow: Compute next HS duties (+10% with wrap).
Commit shadow registers atomically with IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_applyUpdate() within the same module.
api_boundary_clarification.separation_note: Timer setup and commit are owned by this PWM module; external code uses only GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_init() and GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update() to avoid tight coupling.

driver_changes: Replace legacy IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl paired driver with unified multi-channel IfxGtm_Pwm for six single-ended channels, keeping a shared IfxGtm_Tom_Timer time base.
api_changes: IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl_init → IfxGtm_Pwm_init (6x channels in one instance) + IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_init (shared time base)
IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl_setOnTime → IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate (HS); LS via inverted polarity (no dead-time)
migration_notes.context_preserved: The solution retains the exact pin routing to P02.x TOM0 TOUTs, enforces a shared center-aligned 20 kHz time base, initializes HS duties to 25/50/75%, applies complementary LS behavior with zero dead-time, and performs synchronized +10% duty updates every 500 ms with wrap—matching the original motor-control intent.
high_level_sequence: Startup: Cpu0_Main calls GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_init(), which configures and owns the IfxGtm_Tom_Timer (20 kHz, center-aligned) and initializes all six channels via IfxGtm_Pwm. Channels are started in sync.
Trigger: A 500 ms scheduler tick (e.g., STM/OS alarm or main loop polling) invokes GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_update(). No GTM interrupt is required.

Data flow: Update() computes next HS duties (+10% with wrap) -> writes via IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate() -> commits with IfxGtm_Tom_Timer_applyUpdate() for atomic shadow transfer -> TOM outputs take effect on the next time-base update.
